question: Brajesh Kumar Singh was recently appointed as MD and CEO of Canara Bank. Which bank was he serving as Executive Director before this appointment, and what is his tenure end date?
options: D) Union Bank of India — 30 April 2028
explanation: Brajesh Kumar Singh was Executive Director of Indian Bank before being appointed MD & CEO of Canara Bank (HQ: Bengaluru). Tenure: till 30 April 2029 (superannuation). He succeeds K. Satyanarayana Raju. Bank of Baroda, PNB, Union Bank are distractors from the PSB space. #02June2026

question: Vice Admiral Vineet McCarty assumed charge as the 20th Commander-in-Chief of the Andaman and Nicobar Command (ANC). Which of the following statements about ANC is INCORRECT?
options: B) Its headquarters is at Sri Vijaya Puram
C) It is India's first and only Integrated Theatre Command
D) It operates under the exclusive command of the Indian Navy
answer: D) It operates under the exclusive command of the Indian Navy
explanation: ANC is NOT exclusively under the Navy — it is an Integrated Theatre Command where Army, Navy, and Air Force work under ONE commander for a geographical/operational objective. Established 2001, HQ: Sri Vijaya Puram. Vineet McCarty succeeded Vice Admiral Ajay Kochhar (became Vice Chief of Naval Staff). #02June2026

question: Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh was given additional charge of DRDO Chairman. Why was this additional charge necessitated?
options: A) The previous Chairman resigned citing personal reasons
B) The extended tenure of incumbent DRDO Chairman Samir V Kamat ended
C) The post was vacant due to the elevation of previous Chairman to Cabinet rank
D) DRDO was merged with the Ministry of Defence under a restructuring order
answer: B) The extended tenure of incumbent DRDO Chairman Samir V Kamat ended
explanation: The additional charge was given because the extended tenure of incumbent DRDO Chairman Samir V Kamat ended. DRDO (est. 1958, HQ: New Delhi) functions under Ministry of Defence. Resignation, Cabinet elevation, and restructuring are fabricated distractors. #02June2026

question: India's IIP grew 4.9% in April 2026. What makes this data release uniquely significant compared to previous months?
options: A) It is the first IIP data released under base year 2004-05
B) It is the first IIP data released under base year 2011-12
C) It is the first IIP data released under new base year 2022-23
D) It is the first IIP data released under base year 2017-18
answer: C) It is the first IIP data released under new base year 2022-23
explanation: April 2026 IIP (4.9% growth) is the first data released under the new base year 2022-23, replacing the old 2011-12 series. Released by MoSPI. The new series expanded the item basket to 463 items. Base years 2004-05, 2011-12, and 2017-18 are distractors referencing older or non-existent revisions. #02June2026

question: GST collection for May 2026 was ₹1.94 lakh crore. Which month recorded the all-time high GST collection and what was that figure?
explanation: April 2026 recorded the all-time high GST collection of ₹2.43 lakh crore (8.7% YoY growth). May 2026 was ₹1.94 lakh crore (3.2% YoY). Chart data: Jan ₹1.93L, Feb ₹1.83L, Mar ₹1.78L, Apr ₹2.43L, May ₹1.94L (est). All other months are distractors using actual chart values. #02June2026

question: UPI hit a record high in May 2026 with 23.2 billion transactions. NPCI, which oversees UPI, is a joint initiative of which two bodies?
options: A) SEBI and Ministry of Finance
B) RBI and Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
D) Ministry of Finance and DPIIT
answer: B) RBI and Indian Banks' Association (IBA)
explanation: NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India) is a joint initiative of RBI and the Indian Banks' Association (IBA). It is an umbrella body for retail payment & settlement systems. UPI processed 23.2 billion transactions worth ₹29.90 lakh crore in May 2026 — a record high. SEBI, NITI Aayog, DPIIT have no role in NPCI's establishment. #02June2026

question: D K Shivakumar was recently sworn in as the Chief Minister of Karnataka. What is his position in the sequence of Karnataka Chief Ministers?

question: The AI-enabled CPGRAMS Voice Chatbot 'SAMADHAN DIDI' was developed by DARPG in collaboration with which platform?
explanation: SAMADHAN DIDI was developed by DARPG in collaboration with BHASHINI (BHASHa INterface for India), India's AI-powered language translation platform under MeitY. DigiLocker, UMANG, and GeM are other digital govt platforms unrelated to this chatbot. #04June2026

question: The CPGRAMS Voice Chatbot 'SAMADHAN DIDI' currently supports how many Scheduled Languages of India?
explanation: SAMADHAN DIDI supports all 22 Scheduled Languages of India, making the public grievance system multilingual and citizen-friendly. CPGRAMS stands for Centralized Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System. Options A, B, and D are incorrect counts. #04June2026

question: Navachar Mantra, a flagship national programme for grassroots innovators, was launched by which Union Minister?
explanation: Jayant Chaudhary, Union Minister for Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, launched Navachar Mantra to identify, mentor and scale grassroots innovations. Dharmendra Pradhan handles Education; others hold different portfolios. #04June2026

question: The Union Cabinet approved a ₹10,000 crore ATF Price Stabilisation Fund. What is the duration of this support scheme?
explanation: The ATF Price Stabilisation Fund has a duration of 36 months (3 years) or until the advance amount is fully recovered, whichever is earlier. The fund is self-sustaining and revolving in nature, meant to shield Indian airlines from fuel price volatility due to the West Asia crisis. #04June2026

question: The ₹10,000 crore ATF Price Stabilisation Fund was approved to be channelled through which entities to provide fuel support to Indian airlines?
options: C) DGCA and Ministry of Civil Aviation
D) National Aviation Company of India
explanation: The support of ₹10,000 crore was approved for Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) to provide ATF price stabilisation to Indian airlines. Airlines must sign an MoU with OMCs and purchase ATF only from participating OMCs. Other options are regulatory or administrative bodies, not fuel supply entities. #04June2026

question: The Investor Education and Protection Fund Authority (IEPFA) organised the 'Niveshak Shivir' in Bhopal in collaboration with which regulatory body?
explanation: IEPFA, under the Ministry of Corporate Affairs, organised the day-long 'Niveshak Shivir' in Bhopal in collaboration with SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India). The event served as a one-stop platform for resolving issues related to unclaimed dividends and shares. #04June2026

question: India received the fourth squadron of the S-400 Triumf Air Defence System. The original procurement agreement with Russia was signed in which year?
explanation: The $5.43 billion (approx. ₹40,000 crore+) India-Russia agreement for five S-400 squadrons was signed in 2018. The S-400 Triumf is a Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile (SAM) system. India received the fourth of five contracted squadrons in 2026. #04June2026

title: Government to Replace WPI with Producer Price Index Over Five Years
content:
🏛️ India's Inflation Measurement Reform — WPI to PPI Transition

👤 Who → Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

📋 What → India begins a five-year transition from Wholesale Price Index (WPI) to Producer Price Index (PPI)

📍 Where → India (national policy)

🎯 Purpose → To modernise India's inflation measurement framework with a more comprehensive producer-level price index

📌 Important Facts
▪️ Revised WPI series with base year 2022-23 to be released on 15 June 2026 (replacing 2011-12 base)
▪️ Revised WPI basket expands from 697 to 957 commodities (adding solar energy, wind energy, nuclear electricity)
▪️ New indices introduced: Output PPI, Trial Input PPI, Service PPI (7 sectors: banking, insurance, pension fund management, securities transactions, railways, air passenger transport, telecommunications)
▪️ WPI and PPI published simultaneously for 5 years; after that WPI discontinued, PPI becomes primary
▪️ PPI captures prices at producer level (farm-gate/factory-gate); includes goods AND services
▪️ Output PPI covers 123 product categories (~1,500 items initially)

⚠️ Exam Angle → Base year change (2011-12 to 2022-23), commodity count (697 to 957), revised WPI launch date (15 June 2026), and WPI vs PPI distinction are high-probability MCQ topics

explanation: ILO won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1969. It was founded on 11 April 1919, HQ in Geneva, Switzerland, and is led by Director-General Gilbert Houngbo. The ILC (114th edition in 2026) is the annual supreme decision-making body of the ILO. #11June2026

question: Axis Bank increased its stake in Axis Max Life Insurance to what percentage in June 2026?
explanation: Axis Bank raised its stake in Axis Max Life Insurance to 19.99% from an earlier 19.02%, with an additional investment of up to ₹380 crore. The shareholding includes Axis Bank, Axis Securities Ltd, and Axis Capital Ltd. Axis Bank (formerly UTI Bank) was founded on 3 Dec 1993; MD & CEO: Amitabh Chaudhry; Chairperson: P N Prasad. #11June2026

question: Viyona Fintech India received certification as a Biller Operating Unit (BOU) under which payment system?
explanation: Viyona Fintech India, an NPCI-certified Technology Service Provider, received BOU certification under BBPS operated by NPCI Bharat BillPay Limited. As a certified BOU, it can onboard billers onto Bharat BillPay. UPI, NEFT, and IMPS are separate payment systems not related to BOU certification. #11June2026

question: Meta and Reliance Industries partnered to develop a 168 MW AI data centre at which location in India?
explanation: Meta and RIL partnered to develop a 168 MW AI-enabled data centre in Jamnagar, Gujarat — India's first Built-to-Suit (BTS) Data Centre for Meta, to be completed within two years. Llama (Large Language Model Meta AI) is Meta's AI model family. BTS means built specifically for one customer. #11June2026

question: Which three foreign universities received Letters of Approval to establish campuses in India in June 2026?
explanation: Ministry of Education issued LoAs to University of Bristol (UK), University of York (UK), and University of New South Wales — UNSW (Australia) in the presence of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. This aligns with NEP 2020's recommendation to allow top-ranked foreign universities to set up campuses in India. #11June2026

question: Major Prabhat Mishra of the Indian Army won which two awards at the US Army Command and General Staff College (CGSC)?
options: A) Eisenhower Award and Patton Medal
B) Birrer-Brookes Award and General Douglas MacArthur Military Leadership Writing Award
C) Leavenworth Award and Marshall Medal
D) Pentagon Excellence Award and CGSC Gold Medal
answer: B) Birrer-Brookes Award and General Douglas MacArthur Military Leadership Writing Award
explanation: Major Prabhat Mishra won the Birrer-Brookes Award (for Outstanding Master of Military Arts and Science Thesis) and the General Douglas MacArthur Military Leadership Writing Award (for excellence in military leadership writing) at CGSC, Fort Leavenworth, Kansas, USA. Other options are fictitious. #11June2026

question: The first test flight of the 'Made in India' Airbus C295 military transport aircraft was conducted from the Final Assembly Line at which city?
explanation: Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL) and Airbus completed the first test flight of the maiden Made-in-India C295 from the Final Assembly Line in Vadodara. Deal: Sept 2021 for 56 aircraft at ₹21,935 crore; first 16 from Spain, remaining 40 made in India by TASL for the IAF. #11June2026

question: The Ministry of Defence signed a ₹449 crore contract with which company for procurement of GNSS Jammers for the Indian Navy?
options: B) Accord Software and Systems Pvt Ltd, Bengaluru
answer: B) Accord Software and Systems Pvt Ltd, Bengaluru
explanation: MoD signed a ₹449 crore contract with Accord Software and Systems Pvt Ltd, Bengaluru, for 20 Enhanced Capability GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) Jammers for the Indian Navy. A satellite jammer is an electronic warfare system that disrupts/blocks enemy satellite navigation signals. BEL, DRDO, and L&T are plausible but incorrect here. #11June2026

question: The 2nd edition of the Archery Premier League (APL) 2026 is scheduled to be held at which venue in Hyderabad?
explanation: The 2nd APL will be held at People's Plaza, Necklace Road, Hyderabad, Telangana from 8–18 October 2026. APL debuted in Delhi in 2025. Six franchise teams: Mighty Marathas, Rajputana Royals, Prithviraaj Yodhas, Kakatiya Knights, Chero Archers, Chola Chiefs. #11June2026

question: Tamil film director Bharathiraja, who passed away in June 2026, was popularly known by which title?
options: A) Father of Tamil New Wave Cinema
B) Father of Rural Cinema in Tamil Nadu
C) Pioneer of Parallel Cinema in South India
D) Father of Commercial Tamil Cinema
answer: B) Father of Rural Cinema in Tamil Nadu
explanation: Bharathiraja was called the 'Father of Rural Cinema in Tamil Nadu' for bringing rural life, village culture, and realistic storytelling to mainstream Tamil cinema. He was awarded the Padma Shri in 2004 (India's 4th highest civilian honour). He passed away at 84. #11June2026

question: The International Day of Play was first officially observed in which year, and what is the 2026 theme?
explanation: The International Day of Play was officially started on June 11, 2024, established by the United Nations General Assembly. The 2026 theme is 'Protect Play, Protect Childhood'. It is observed every year on June 11 to champion every child's right to play. Other year-theme combinations are incorrect. #11June2026
